thesis: Grid and electrical components supplier tied to utility and data center infrastructure without the same hype level as the hottest cooling names.
downsideTrigger: Utility order slowdown, margin pressure, or guidance that implies electrical demand is normalizing.

thesis: Outsourced assembly and test exposure to advanced packaging demand as AI chips require more complex integration.
downsideTrigger: Weak margins, capex burden, slower advanced packaging ramp, or customer inventory correction.

thesis: Infrastructure contractor for grid, power, and utility construction, benefiting from multi-year data center and electrification spend.
downsideTrigger: Execution risk, labor cost pressure, delayed projects, or backlog growth falling below revenue growth.

thesis: Electrical enclosures, protection, and liquid cooling exposure for data center infrastructure.
downsideTrigger: Acquisition integration risk, slower data center orders, or valuation compressing after a re-rating.

thesis: Optical components and transceiver exposure as large AI clusters require faster data movement.
downsideTrigger: Customer concentration, pricing pressure, supply catch-up, or datacom growth below expectations.

thesis: Thermal management and data center cooling exposure as rack densities rise.
downsideTrigger: Margin pressure from capacity expansion, order lumpiness, or guidance failing to support the rerating.

thesis: High-speed connectivity and active electrical cable exposure to AI cluster networking.
downsideTrigger: Very high expectations, customer concentration, or any sign of AI connectivity order digestion.

thesis: Azure and AI infrastructure capex are master demand signals for the rest of the chain.
downsideTrigger: Capex slowdown, weaker Azure growth, or AI monetization failing to justify investment.

thesis: Dominant AI accelerator platform and the first obvious beneficiary of AI capex.
downsideTrigger: Gross margin peak, export restrictions, customer digestion, or competition from custom silicon.

thesis: HBM and DRAM supplier benefiting from AI memory scarcity.
downsideTrigger: Memory pricing rollover, HBM share loss, or capex-driven oversupply risk.

thesis: Data center Ethernet switching supplier for AI cluster networking.
downsideTrigger: Cloud titan digestion, margin normalization, or AI Ethernet share disappointment.

thesis: GTM data and AI sales intelligence are part of the software ROI proof stage, but execution must show durable growth.
downsideTrigger: Weak retention, slower enterprise demand, or AI features failing to improve sales productivity.
